title: quercus faginea var. broteroi
intro: Quercus faginea var. broteroi, also known as the Portuguese oak or Brotero's oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus faginea var. broteroi include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to support overall well-being and alleviate specific health concerns.
